scope: v1 default — no talking head, no studio, no motion-graphics designer. Every primitive is renderable from our DB + a small static asset library.

source_reference_videos.title: 20 Easy Faceless Niches Explained in 17 Minutes

systems.label: Two-background semantic system
systems.rule: Every frame lives in ONE of two background modes; viewer subconsciously knows where they are (narration vs proof) from bg color alone.
systems.violations_to_avoid: putting narration text on dark gray bg, putting YT data on white bg — breaks the signaling

systems.label: Card-with-padding composition
systems.rule: No content fills the frame edge-to-edge. Every YT screenshot, thumbnail, video clip is a padded card sitting on the background. The padding IS the layout.

render_spec.inner_card: centered, max 80-85% of frame width, rounded corners 8-16px

systems.label: Typographic hierarchy + color semantics within cards
systems.rule: Text within a card has hierarchy by size + weight + color. Key word emphasized, connectors neutral. Color is semantic.

yellow.meaning: highlight / 'look here' — reserved for circles/backgrounds, NOT inline text

white.meaning: neutral narration on dark gray bg

systems.principle: Visual equivalent of vocal stress — delivers the emphasis a talking head would convey with voice inflection.

systems.label: Line-drawing characters as faceless-host proxy
systems.rule: Stylized line-drawing icons substitute for the host's facial expressions. Every emotional/reaction beat → an icon.
systems.principle: The icon library is the host's face decomposed into reusable reactions. Without this, the video has no emotional register.

systems.label: YouTube native visual language as the video's own
systems.rule: All proof-side visuals must visually match YouTube's actual rendering — channel pages, About pages, thumbnail cards ('29M views • 7 months ago'), search results, rounded-rectangle mini-player frames for content clips.
systems.principle: Authenticity by mimicry. Viewers instinctively trust what looks like YouTube. Removes need for custom motion-graphics.

primitives.label: Line-drawing icon library (host-reaction proxies)

icons.use: direct viewer address, 'this is for YOU'

primitives.render_spec: flat single-color SVG (black on white bg / white on dark bg). Consistency of style across icons is mandatory — must look like the same line-drawing set.

primitives.label: 'Most popular video' isolated callout card
primitives.trigger: speech references a specific top video (e.g. 'their most popular video has more than 1 million views')

render_spec.content: single thumbnail centered with YT-native layout: thumbnail image + title text below + 'X views • N ago' timestamp
render_spec.size_ratio: ~60% of card area (larger than grid items at ~30%)

primitives.distinct_from: regular thumbnail cards in the per-niche proof grid

primitives.label: Yellow-circle / highlight annotation on element within a screenshot
primitives.trigger: speech references a specific element visible in the on-screen screenshot (e.g. subs number, view count, policy bullet)

render_spec.overlay: SVG circle/ring/box on top of screenshot
annotation_types: background fill behind a number or word

primitives.label: Inline color highlighting within a text card
primitives.trigger: emphasize a specific phrase within an otherwise-neutral text card

render_spec.size_rule: same font size as surrounding text — NOT bigger (bigger is the money-shot full-card pattern)

primitives.distinct_from: money_shot_card (where the colored phrase is the whole-card content at much larger size)

primitives.label: Chalkboard concept tag
primitives.trigger: emphasize the load-bearing conceptual word of a niche segment

render_spec.background: dark green or black chalkboard texture

primitives.frequency_rule: at most once per niche segment — overusing dilutes the signal

excluded_patterns.rationale: Class A signals. Including any of these in Class B output breaks the indie-faceless aesthetic.

composition_example.scenario: Single niche reveal segment showing the systems + primitives in sequence

sequence.content: YT channel page + yellow circle on subs
shrug character + 'If we assume' + '$6 RPM' (green inline)
Their most popular video — 1.3M views • 1 month ago
cat-with-thumbs-up + 'if you're watching this far, I appreciate it'

asset_library_one_time_cost: HTML/CSS templates: YT channel-page mock, YT About-page mock, YT thumbnail card, YT search-results mock
render_feasibility.render_stack: HTML + CSS + static SVG. Backgrounds = solid colors. Text overlays = standard typography. Screenshots = templated and DB-fed.

next_checkpoints: audio-sfx-class-b — corpus mining of audio_description for music + SFX vocabulary
slot-rendering-class-b — for each data-points.json slot, which primitives deliver it
visual-packaging-class-a — brand-studio grammar when we decide to build it
visual-packaging-class-c — Hindi listicle grammar after Hindi corpus mining
